scope: Paid (Google Ads) visits only. US visitors. Internal/test visits excluded.
method: Based on PostHog session recordings for paid (Google Ads) visitors: each visit's start time, landing page, clicks, form activity and outcome. Anything unusual (such as an odd click pattern) is flagged for review and confirmed before being reported as an issue, rather than assumed.

posthog.filter: Paid Search channel, US, test visits excluded

reconciliation.note: June 10 recorded 2 paid visits against 1 paid click - most likely the late-night visit counting on June 11 in the Google Ads account time zone. To be confirmed.

sessions.label: Visit 1
sessions.verdict: Browsed the page but didn't start or submit the form.
flags: High click count with no form typing - flagged for a closer look before drawing any conclusion about page friction.

sessions.label: Visit 2
sessions.verdict: Arrived on the page but didn't interact - no clicks and no form activity (a bounce).
sessions.note: A tab left open late on June 10 (US Central).

insights.headline: 22 paid clicks, 17 recorded visits, 2 form submissions this week
insights.body: From June 4-10, Google Ads recorded 22 paid clicks. PostHog captured 17 paid visits (about 77% of clicks). Two of those visits submitted the contact form - roughly 1 in 9 recorded paid visits.

insights.headline: Only the visitors who filled in the form converted
insights.body: 15 of 17 recorded paid visits involved no form typing at all. The two submissions (June 7 and June 9) were the only visits where the form was actually filled in.

insights.headline: Two days had paid clicks but no recorded visits
insights.body: June 4 (3 clicks) and June 6 (1 click) had no recorded paid visits. Recording coverage is uneven, so daily visit counts can sit below daily click counts.

insights.headline: June 10 shows more visits than clicks
insights.body: Most days have fewer recorded visits than clicks, but June 10 shows 2 visits against 1 paid click - most likely the late-night visit counting on June 11 in the Google Ads account's time zone. Flagged to confirm.

insights.headline: One visit is flagged for a closer look
insights.body: The June 10 visit with a high click count and no form typing (a tab left open for several hours) is flagged for review before any conclusion is drawn about page friction.

data_notes: Window: June 4-10, 2026 (US Central), matching the conversion dashboard's last-7-days view.
Each visit is dated by its exact start time in US Central time.
Scope: paid (Google Ads) visits only, US visitors, internal and test visits excluded.
Privacy: visitor names and emails are not shown. Visits are referenced by anonymous labels and recording links only.
Submissions counted: 2 (June 7 and June 9), both matched to the paid leads in the conversion dashboard. Phone taps and friction are reported as 0 - they are only counted once confirmed.
Paid-click counts come from the conversion dashboard (3, 3, 1, 4, 5, 5, 1 = 22 for June 4-10).
headline_state: Paid visitor sessions for June 4-10 (US Central). 22 paid clicks, 17 recorded visits (about 77% captured), 2 form submissions (June 7 and June 9). 15 of 17 visits involved no form typing. June 10 shows 2 visits vs 1 click (time-zone check pending); one visit flagged for review.
